title: Tension monitor for undercarriage track in a work machine
abstract: A work machine (100), such as a tractor or skid steer, has a motion sensor (204) attached to a segment (132) of a continuous ground-engaging track (110) in its undercarriage (105). The motion sensor (204) generates motion data indicative of a change in motion for the track segment as the track (110) rotates around a track assembly (108). An electronic controller (102) for the work machine (100) receives and evaluates the motion data, first to identify a location of the track segment (132) within the undercarriage (105) and then to compare the motion data with expected motion data for the track segment (132) under normal track tension. Motion data outside a range of the expected motion data indicates an abnormal sag in the track (110), causing the electronic controller (102) to generate an alert for adjustment of the track tension.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A system for monitoring tension in an endless track (110) of a machine (100), comprising: a drive sprocket (112) having teeth (126) around a circular periphery; an idler (114) having an outer circumference; an endless track (110) engaged with the teeth (126) and with the outer circumference for movement at least over a span between the drive sprocket (112) and the idler (114); a motion sensor (204), attached to a segment (132) of the endless track (110), configured to generate track data indicative of a change in motion by the motion sensor (204) over the span; a memory configured to store target data representative of an expected change in motion by the motion sensor (204) over the span; a controller (102) configured to determine that the track data is not within a range of the target data; and an actuator configured to generate an alert in response to the track data not being within the range of the target data. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the endless track (110) comprises a continuous chain (123) formed from track links (124) joined together by track pins (308), the motion sensor (204) being attached to one of the track links (124). 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the motion sensor (204) is embedded within a cavity (322) in one of the track links (124). 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the endless track (110) comprises track shoes (128) attached to track links (124), the track links (124) being joined together by track pins (308), the motion sensor (204) being 

embedded within a cavity (324, 326) in one of the track shoes (128) or in one of the track pins (308). 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the motion sensor (204) is a gyroscope and the track data comprises an angular rate of motion for the motion sensor (204). 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the motion sensor (204) is an accelerometer and the motion comprises a change in velocity for the motion sensor (204). 7. A computer-implemented method of monitoring tension in a track (110) within an undercarriage (105) of a work machine (100), comprising: receiving, by a processor (102), motion data from a motion sensor (204) within a track (110) segment during rotation of the track (110) about a track assembly (108) in the undercarriage (105); comparing, by the processor (102), a portion of the motion data indicative of when the track segment was at a predetermined location offset from a drive sprocket (112) with target motion data for the predetermined location stored in a memory; determining that the portion of the motion data is not compliant with the target motion data; and generating an alert relating to tension for the track (110). 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, further comprising: determining, by the processor (102) and based at least in part on the motion data, when the track segment is at the predetermined location. 

 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the determining when the motion sensor (204) is at the predetermined location comprises: identifying, by the processor (102), when the track segment passes over the drive sprocket (112) based on changes in motion indicated by the motion data. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, further comprising: receiving, by the processor (102), initial motion data from the motion sensor (204) during a previous rotation of the track (110) about the track assembly (108); and storing at least some of the initial motion data as the target motion data in the memory.
